rejected_candidates.title: Share Price Calculation Error Causes User Fund Loss in instantWithdraw
rejected_candidates.reason: `instantWithdraw()` using the lower of current and last-settled price is a conservative pricing rule, not a standalone exploit. The real reportable issue is the separate burn-before-liquidity-check bug already captured in F-001.

rejected_candidates.title: Fee Not Applied to Share-Based Withdrawals
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. The withdrawal fee is applied to `actualWithdrawn` after both the amount-based and share-based branches, so share-based instant withdrawals do pay fees.

rejected_candidates.title: Lack of Slippage Protection in Rebalancing
rejected_candidates.reason: Too speculative and strategy-dependent. The controller just routes deposits and withdrawals; no concrete protocol-level loss is shown from the code provided.

rejected_candidates.title: Unlimited Owner Control Over Token Transfers
rejected_candidates.reason: This is the intended owner-controlled pause mechanism (`enable`), not an implementation flaw.

rejected_candidates.title: Single-Point Proposal Role Modification
rejected_candidates.reason: Governance centralization/trust-model issue only. No bug beyond the privileged role already being powerful by design.

rejected_candidates.title: Migration Without User Consent
rejected_candidates.reason: Governance migration authority is explicit design, not a code vulnerability absent a stronger implementation bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Division by Zero in VaultMath
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. `VaultMath` guards on `_assetPerShare > 1`; the cited total-supply scenario is unrelated to these pure math helpers.

rejected_candidates.title: Daily Quota Can Be Fully Consumed by Single User
rejected_candidates.reason: This is the documented behavior of a global daily cap, not a vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Return Value in Strategy Withdrawal
rejected_candidates.reason: The cited contract is an abstract base with virtual stubs, not deployed logic. No concrete vulnerable implementation is shown.

rejected_candidates.title: AssetsVault Can Be Reinitialized
rejected_candidates.reason: `setNewVault()` is an intended privileged migration hook and depends on already-authorized callers; this is not a separate bug.

rejected_candidates.title: No Access Control on Stone Contract Initialization
rejected_candidates.reason: Constructor initialization is only performed at deployment. Setting an undesirable initial cap is configuration risk, not a vulnerability.
